report_name: Global "Disruption Week" Dismantles Over 1.4 Million Accounts in Cybercrime Crackdown

threat_actors.name: Southeast Asia Scam Networks
threat_actors.description: Industrial-scale scam networks operating out of compounds in Southeast Asia, often using trafficked and forced labor for large-scale fraud operations. This entity represents the collective criminal groups targeted during the 'Disruption Week' operation.
threat_actors.goals: Large-scale financial fraud and human trafficking for forced labor in scam operations.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing via Service
attack_patterns.description: The scam networks used social media platforms like Facebook and Instagram to send unsolicited messages with fraudulent high-paying job offers to lure individuals into trafficking and fraud schemes.

tools.name: Starlink
tools.description: Starlink satellite internet kits were used by the cybercriminal networks for internet connectivity in their remote compounds.

infrastructure.name: Malicious Social Media Accounts
infrastructure.description: Over 1.4 million malicious accounts, pages, and groups on platforms like Facebook and Instagram used to conduct large-scale fraud operations and lure victims.

infrastructure.name: Scam Network Servers
infrastructure.description: Malicious servers and hosting infrastructure used by the scam networks, which were decommissioned during the 'Disruption Week' operation.
